title: Sepsis review documentation

items.final_wording: Supports suspected-infection and fever presentation documentation, source symptoms, risk factors, and recognition that sepsis can present with nonspecific illness.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management
source.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1–1.2 — recognition, suspected infection, source, and risk factors

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports suspected-infection and fever presentation documentation, source symptoms, risk factors, and recognition that sepsis can present with nonspecific illness.

items.rationale: Supports suspected infection, source and risk-factor documentation for ICU sepsis review; no clinical action is generated.

items.final_wording: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management
source.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence

exact_location.heading: Recommendation 1.3 — history, observations, circulation, respiration, skin, urine output, and source examination

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.

items.rationale: Supports entered physiological observations and source examination for ICU sepsis review; no clinical action is generated.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-selected blood and microbiology testing, urinalysis, chest radiography, and abdominal or pelvic imaging to identify an infection source.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management
source.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.8 and 1.11 — source identification and investigations

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-selected blood and microbiology testing, urinalysis, chest radiography, and abdominal or pelvic imaging to identify an infection source.

items.rationale: Supports clinician-selected source investigations for ICU sepsis review; no clinical action is generated.

Legacy item actions remain provisional until the full text is compared item-by-item.
